content: DSM-5-TR criteria: Manic episode requires abnormally elevated/expansive/irritable mood and increased energy/activity for ≥1 week (or any duration if hospitalized), plus ≥3 symptoms (grandiosity, decreased sleep need, pressured speech, flight of ideas, distractibility, increased activity, excessive risky activities) causing marked impairment. Hypomanic episode: similar symptoms for ≥4 consecutive days with unequivocal change in functioning but not markedly impairing. Bipolar I: ≥1 manic episode (depressive episode common but not required). Bipolar II: ≥1 hypomanic AND ≥1 major depressive episode, no history of mania. Cyclothymic disorder: ≥2 years of hypomanic and depressive symptoms not meeting full episode criteria. Specifiers include current episode type, severity, psychotic features, and rapid cycling (≥4 episodes/year).
